bibliographic_data.title: Isolated singularities for elliptic equations with convolution terms in a punctured ball
bibliographic_data.abstract: The purpose of this article is two-fold. First, we investigate the inequality \(-\Delta u + V(x)u \geq f\) in \(B_1 \setminus \{0\} \subset R^N, N \geq 2\), where \(f \in L_{loc}^1(B_1).\) If \(V \geq 0\) is radially symmetric, we provide optimal conditions for which any solution \(0 \leq u \in C^2(B_1 \setminus \{0\})\) of the above inequality satisfies \(u, \Delta u, V(x)u \in L_{loc}^1(B_1).\) This extends a result of H. Brezis and P.-L. Lions (1982), originally established for constant potentials \(V\). Second, we investigate the equation \(-\Delta u + \lambda V(x)u = (K_{\alpha,\beta} * u^p)u^q\) in \(B_1 \setminus \{0\}\), where \(0 \leq V \in C^{0,\nu}(B_1 \setminus \{0\}), 0 < \nu < 1, \lambda, p, q > 0\) and \(K_{\alpha,\beta}(x) = |x|^{-\alpha} \log^\beta (2e/|x|).\) For \(N \geq 3\), we establish sharp conditions on the exponents \(\alpha, \beta, p, q\) under which singular solutions exist and exhibit the asymptotic behavior \(u(x) \asymp |x|^{2-N}\) near the origin. For \(N = 2\), we provide a classification of the existence and boundedness of solutions based on the local behavior of the potential \(V(x)\) near the origin.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper extends Brezis and Lions' result to a more general case involving convolution terms and provides sharp conditions for singular solutions in the context of non-local equations.
core_content.key_methodology: The authors use distribution theory and properties of fundamental solutions to derive optimal conditions for the existence and behavior of solutions near singularities.
outcomes: Optimal conditions for the inequality \(-\Delta u + V(x)u \geq f\) in a punctured ball are provided, extending Brezis and Lions' result.
Sharp conditions on exponents for the existence of singular solutions to non-local equations involving convolution terms are established.
core_content.full_text_markdown: The study focuses on semi-linear elliptic equations with isolated singularities. The authors extend a fundamental result by Brezis and Lions (1982) to more general cases, particularly those involving convolution terms. They investigate the inequality \(-\Delta u + V(x)u \geq f\) in a punctured ball \(B_1 \setminus \{0\}\), where \(f \in L_{loc}^1(B_1)\). For radially symmetric potentials, they provide optimal conditions ensuring that any solution \(u \in C^2(B_1 \setminus \{0\})\) satisfies \(u, \Delta u, V(x)u \in L_{loc}^1(B_1)\). Additionally, the paper explores non-local equations of the form \(-\Delta u + \lambda V(x)u = (K_{\alpha,\beta} * u^p)u^q\), where \(V\) is a continuous function with certain properties. For dimensions \(N \geq 3\), they derive sharp conditions on the exponents to ensure the existence of singular solutions and their asymptotic behavior near the origin. In two dimensions, they classify the existence and boundedness of solutions based on the local behavior of the potential.
